Association for Legal Justice (ALJ) Human Rights Testimony, Northern Ireland
Please cite any data used from this data project as follows:
Loyle, Cyanne E.;Davenport, Christian;Sullivan, Christopher. 2018. "Association for Legal Justice (ALJ) Human Rights Testimony, Northern Ireland". Qualitative Data Repository. https://doi.org/10.5064/F6LHMHJR

The Association of Legal Justice (ALJ) was a human rights organization formed in 1970 to catalogue the abuses suffered by residents of Northern Ireland during the armed conflict. ALJ disbanded in the early 1990s. The organization primarily focused on charging and sentencing patterns in the Northern Ireland justice system at the time. During their history, ALJ collected over 4,000 statements of abuse from victims or those who witnessed the violations. These statements were collected with a wide range of detail and identified both violent as well as non-violent events during the conflict, particularly in regards to police and state abuse. NIRI𠏋 collection of statements were compiled from both the personal collection of Clara O㰕eilly, one of the founding members of ALJ, as well as the Father Murray Collection in the Tomas O𤦔iaich Library at Saint Patrick𠏋 Cathedral, Armagh, Northern Ireland.

Standard access for all files listed below.

This collection contains the following files:

1.	NIRI_ALJ_Permission_to_NIRI.pdf
2.	NIRI_Data_Narrative.pdf
3.	NIRI_QDR_File_Processing_Note.pdf
4.	README_NIRI.txt

DATA PROCESSING NOTE

The data were processed according to the Qualitative Data Repository's curation policy. All files were checked for integrity. Files were checked to make sure they match documentation and consistent file names were applied. Files underwent OCR and partial de-identification and were converted from PDF formats to PDF/A using Adobe Acrobat Pro DC Version 2018.009.20050, XMP tags edited using AutoMedata version 1.0.0.20. See 0_NIRI_QDR File Processing Note.pdf for more details. 


*****


These data were obtained from the Qualitative Data Repository (QDR - http://qdr.syr.edu), a dedicated archive for storing and sharing digital data (and accompanying documentation) generated or collected through qualitative and multi-method research in the social sciences. QDR is supported by the National Science Foundation. Please consult QDR's General Terms and Conditions of Use (https://qdr.syr.edu/termsandconditions).
